channel.title: Combined Politepol Feed
channel.description: Merged feed from multiple Politepol sources

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskiy arrives in Jordan for next leg of Gulf tour
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy arrived in Jordan ​on Sunday as part of a ‌Gulf tour aimed at shoring up defence ties with countries in the region amid ​rising tensions over the Iran ​war. Zelenskiy is seeking support from Gulf ⁠states for Ukraine's war against Russia ​as Western mili

item.title: France detains two more suspects over foiled Paris Bank of America attack
item.description: French authorities have arrested two more suspects over a ​foiled attack on Bank of America’s Paris offices, the country’s anti-terrorism prosecutor’s office said ‌on Sunday. “Two further individuals were taken into police custody last night as part of the investigatio

item.title: Russia says Ukraine controls 15–17 per cent of Donetsk region
item.description: The Russian-installed head of ​Ukraine's Donetsk region, ‌Denis Pushilin, told Russian president Vladimir Putin ​at a ​meeting shown on state ⁠TV on Tuesday ​that Ukraine controls around ​15-17 per cent of the region. Russia claims the whole of ​the Donetsk region as part ​of Russia and says

item.title: Greece sees deal to buy Italian-made frigates by April, minister says
item.description: Greece hopes to wrap up talks and sign a deal ​for the purchase of Bergamini class ‌frigates from Italy by April, Defence Minister Nikos Dendias said in an interview ​released on Sunday. High-ranking officials from ​Greece and Italy last year signed memorandums for ⁠naval cooperation, opening

item.title: Slovakia PM Fico to meet EU's von der Leyen to push for restart of Druzhba oil flows
item.description: Slovak Prime Minister Robert Fico said on Sunday he ‌would meet with EU chief Ursula von der Leyen on Tuesday, as Slovakia and Hungary ramp up pressure on Ukraine to restart Russian oil flows, warning Slovakia could block a major EU ​loan to Kyiv. Russian oil flows through the Druzhba pipelin

item.title: Explosion hits US embassy in Oslo, causing minor damage, police say
item.description: The US embassy in Oslo was hit by an explosion early on Sunday, causing minor damage but no injuries, Norwegian police said. A loud blast was heard near the embassy compound in western Oslo at around 1am (0000 GMT), and smoke was seen rising from the area, eyewitnesses told local media. "We've de

item.title: Plane carrying newly printed currency crashes, killing 15
item.description: Lockheed C-130 Hercules cargo plane carrying newly printed Bolivian banknotes crashed Friday near the capital, leaving at least 15 people dead, injuring others and damaging numerous vehicles, officials said. Defense Minister Marcelo Salinas said the aircraft skidded off the runway while landing at

item.title: Buses, trams and trains grind to a halt across Germany at start of 2-day strike
item.description: Buses, trams and trains across Germany ground to a halt early Friday as local transport workers heeded a call by the Verdi public sector union to stage a strike on Feb 27 and 28. The union is aiming to gain leverage in negotiations that cover working conditions, specifically working hours and shif

item.title: Cuba situation escalating, humanitarian needs must be solved: Kremlin
item.description: The Kremlin said on Thursday that the situation in Cuba was heating up and called for restraint after a deadly incident with a Florida-registered speedboat off the coast of the Caribbean island. Cuban forces killed four exiles and wounded six others who sailed into Cuban waters aboard a speedboat

item.title: UK to enforce travel permit requirement on foreign visitors
item.description: Starting on Wednesday, visitors to Britain from 85 countries must obtain an electronic permit in advance of their trip or they will be barred from travelling, the UK interior ministry said. The Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA) scheme requires all visitors who do not need a visa to enter Brita

item.title: Power drought tips Ukraine's economy into worst crisis since war's first year
item.description: Ukraine's economy is enduring its toughest period since the early months of Russia's invasion after sustained air strikes left its power system in tatters as the war enters a fifth year, forcing firms to cut output and shrinking state revenues. From steel mills to miners, cement makers and food pr

item.title: Spain's top court rejects father's bid to halt daughter's euthanasia
item.description: Spain's Constitutional Court has rejected an appeal by the father of a 25‑year‑old paraplegic woman who opposes her access to euthanasia, the court said in a statement on Friday. In 2021, Spain became the fourth European Union country to legalise euthanasia and assisted suicide for people with inc

item.title: Recalled Italian ambassador to return to Switzerland
item.description: Italy’s ambassador to Switzerland will return ​to his post, the ‌Swiss foreign ministry said, months after he was recalled ​in protest over Swiss ​authorities’ handling of a ⁠deadly New Year’s Day ​bar fire in the ​Alpine resort of Crans-Montana. Italy recalled the diplomat

item.title: On Easter, Pope Leo urges world leaders to end wars, renounce conquest
item.description: Pope Leo urged global leaders in his Easter message on Sunday to end the conflicts raging across the world and ​abandon any schemes for power, conquest or domination. The pope, who has emerged ‌as an outspoken critic of the Iran war, lamented in a special message to the thousands gathered in

item.title: Iran says several 'enemy aircraft' destroyed during US pilot rescue mission
item.description: Several aircraft were destroyed during ​the US mission to find a ‌stranded airman in Iran, the Revolutionary Guards said on Sunday ​according to Tasnim news ​agency. "During a joint operation (Aerospace, Ground ⁠Force, Popular Units, Basij ​and Police command), enemy aircraft ​were destroyed,

item.title: US service member missing after Iran shot down jet rescued
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said that a U.S. service member who was missing since Iran shot down a fighter jet has been found in a rescue involving "dozens of aircrafts." The crew member had been missing since Friday, when Iran downed a U.S. F-15E Strike Eagle. A second crew member was rescued ear

item.title: US experts say American strikes on Iran may amount to war crimes
Hopes dim for swift end to Iran war after Trump speech
Trump interested in calling on Arab states to help pay for Iran war, White House says
US resumes embassy operations in Venezuela
Lucrative bets that anticipated Trump's policy surprises warrant scrutiny, experts say
Mexico's navy locates two missing sailboats carrying aid to Cuba
US to continue Iran strikes, pause applies only to energy sites, Semafor reports
Death toll nearly doubles to 66 in Colombian military plane crash
Trump threatens to put ICE agents in airports over funding impasse
Trump hints at wind-down of war as US sends more troops and Iran threatens tourism sites
13 US service members killed in conflict with Iran: report
Four US service members killed in plane crash over Iraq
India faces second year of sugar deficit as mills close early
item.description: India's sugar production is set to fall below consumption for a second straight year, as lower cane yields force mills to close faster than usual, trade officials told Reuters on Thursday. Lower output, coupled with rising exports, is likely to reduce domestic stockpiles and support local prices,

item.title: Myanmar parliament to hold vote to elect president on Friday, house speaker says
item.description: Myanmar's parliament will hold a bicameral vote to elect a president on Friday, house speaker Aung Lin Dwe said on Thursday, with the country's former military chief and junta leader Min Aung Hlaing in the running for the position. The 69-year-old general, who led a 2021 coup that ousted the democ

item.title: Israeli military says strike in Beirut killed Hezbollah southern front commander Haj Youssef Ismail Hashem
item.description: The Israeli military said on Wednesday it had killed a senior Hezbollah official in an overnight attack. Israel's navy killed Haj Youssef Ismail Hashem, the commander of Hezbollah's southern front, the country's military spokesperson Avichay Adraee said in a statement on X. Hezbollah later confir

item.title: Myanmar junta chief nominated for presidential vote
item.description: Myanmar junta chief Min Aung Hlaing was nominated by a lawmaker on Monday for a parliamentary vote that will select the new president of the war-torn Southeast Asian nation, as the powerful veteran general seeks a political role. Min Aung Hlaing, who currently also serves as Myanmar's military chi

item.title: Indonesia accelerates waste-to-energy push in major cities
item.description: Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto has ordered the acceleration of waste-to-energy development, stressing that waste in major cities should be immediately processed into energy as part of efforts to address the country's long-standing waste problem. The directive was delivered during a meeting

item.title: Fire at Korean car parts factory kills 10, injures 59, 4 missing
item.description: A fire broke out around lunchtime at a car parts factory in the South Korean city of Daejeon on Friday, leaving 10 people dead, 25 seriously injured and 34 with minor injuries, the country's safety ministry said on Saturday. Four other workers were still missing, and the government has activated a

item.title: Qatar and Turkey criticise Iran's latest attacks
item.description: Qatari Prime Minister Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman al-Thani and Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan held a joint press conference earlier on Thursday. Al-Thani, who is also Qatar's foreign minister, said that Iran had destroyed Qatar's trust with its "aggression," and that trust needed to be r

item.title: Independent Hong Kong panel starts hearing into devastating inferno
item.description: Human factors rendered ineffective almost all the fire safety measures at the site of a ​Hong Kong blaze that killed 168 people in November, the lead lawyer for a panel led by a judge that ‌is investigating the inferno said on Thursday. The Independent Committee set up by Hong Kong leader Joh

item.title: Iran war: Most civilians killed in UAE strikes were from Bangladesh, Pakistan and Nepal
item.description: Almost all the civilians killed in Iranian missile and drone attacks in the United Arab Emirates were from Pakistan, Bangladesh and Nepal, with only one victim from outside South Asia. Authorities in the UAE have released the names of the six civilians killed in the attacks. The list shows that on

item.title: At least 10 patients killed in hospital fire in India
item.description: At least 10 people were killed in a fire that broke out in the trauma care unit of a hospital in the eastern Indian state of Odisha on Monday, officials said. Five people were critically wounded, but it was not clear if it was from the fire or earlier injuries. The fire was likely to have been

item.title: Israeli foreign minister says no plans for talks with Lebanese government
item.description: Israeli Foreign Minister ​Gideon Saar said on ‌Sunday that the government was not planning to ​hold direct talks ​with Lebanon in the coming ⁠days and had ​not told the United ​States it was running low on missile interceptors. Israel's Haaretz newspaper ​reported on Saturday ​that Israel and

item.title: North Korea fires possible missile towards sea during US-South Korea drills
item.description: North Korea fired a projectile towards the sea on Saturday, South Korea and Japan said, with Tokyo saying it may have been a ballistic missile, while the US and South Korea conducted military drills. The projectile was fired toward the sea off North Korea's east coast, the South Korean military sa
